sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Ὦ δώματ ’ Ἀδμήτειʼ , ἐν οἷς ἔτλην ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Halls of Admetus , wherein I steeled my heart to be content with a servant ’ s board , god though I was . Zeus was to blame ; he slew my son Asclepius , piercing his bosom with a thunderbolt ;

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: οὗ δὴ χολωθεὶς τέκτονας Δίου πυρὸς
sections.translation: whereat I was enraged and smote his Cyclopes , forgers of the heavenly fire ; so my sire in recompense for this forced me to become a slave in a mortal ’ s home . Then came I to this land and kept a stranger ’ s flocks , and to this day have been the saviour of this house .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ὁσίου γὰρ ἀνδρὸς ὅσιος ὢν ἐτύγχανον
sections.translation: For in Pheres ’ son I found a man as holy as myself , and him I saved from death by cheating Destiny , for they promised me , those goddesses of fate , that Admetus should escape the impending doom , if he found a substitute for the powers below .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: πάντας δ ’ ἐλέγξας καὶ διεξελθὼν φίλους ,
sections.translation: So he went through all his list of friends , made trial of each , his father and the aged mother that bare him , [*] but none he found save his wife alone that was willing to die for him and forego the light of life ; she now within the house is upheld in his arms ,

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ψυχορραγοῦσα · τῇδε γάρ σφ ’ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ
sections.translation: gasping out her life ; for to-day is she doomed to die and pass from life to death . But I , for fear pollution overtake me in the house , am leaving the shelter of this roof I love so well , for already I see Death hard by ,

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἱερῆ θανόντων , ὅς νιν εἰς Ἅιδου δόμους
sections.translation: the priest of souls departed , who is on his way to lead her to the halls of Hades ; true to time he comes , watching this day that calls her to her doom .

sections.label: Section 28
sections.original: ἆ ἆ ·
sections.translation: Ha ! What dost thou at this house ? why is it thou art ranging here ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: Φοῖβʼ ; ἀδικεῖς αὖ τιμὰς ἐνέρων
sections.translation: Phoebus ? Once again thou wrongest me , circumscribing and limiting the honours of the nether world . Wert thou not content to hinder the death of Admetus , by thy knavish cunning baulking Destiny ? but now again

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: χέρα τοξήρη φρουρεῖς ὁπλίσας ,
sections.translation: hast thou armed thee with thy bow and art keeping guard o ’ er her , this daughter of Pelias , who undertook , of her free will , to die for her lord and set him free .

sections.label: Section 38
sections.original: θάρσει · δίκην τοι καὶ λόγους κεδνοὺς ἔχω .
sections.translation: Never fear ; I have , be sure , justice and fair pleas to urge .

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: τί δῆτα τόξων ἔργον , εἰ δίκην ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: What has that bow to do , if thou hast justice on thy side ?

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: σύνηθες αἰεὶ ταῦτα βαστάζειν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: ’ Tis my habit ever to carry it .

sections.label: Section 41
sections.original: καὶ τοῖσδέ γ ’ οἴκοις ἐκδίκως προσωφελεῖν .
sections.translation: Ay , and to help this house more than is right .

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: φίλου γὰρ ἀνδρὸς συμφοραῖς βαρύνομαι .
sections.translation: The reason is , I cannot bear a friend ’ s distress .

sections.label: Section 43
sections.original: καὶ νοσφιεῖς με τοῦδε δευτέρου νεκροῦ ;
sections.translation: Wilt rob me of this second corpse likewise ?

sections.label: Section 44
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὐδ ’ ἐκεῖνον πρὸς βίαν σ ’ ἀφειλόμην .
sections.translation: Come ! I did not take the other from thee by violence .

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ὑπὲρ γῆς ἐστι κοὐ κάτω χθονός ;
sections.translation: Then how is it he lives above the earth and not beneath ?

sections.label: Section 46
sections.original: δάμαρτ ’ ἀμείψας , ἣν σὺ νῦν ἥκεις μέτα .
sections.translation: He gave his wife instead , her whom now thou art come to fetch .

sections.label: Section 47
sections.original: κἀπάξομαί γε νερτέρων ὑπὸ χθόνα .
sections.translation: Yea , and I will bear her hence to the nether world .

sections.label: Section 48
sections.original: λαβὼν ἴθʼ · οὐ γὰρ οἶδ ’ ἂν εἰ πείσαιμί σε .
sections.translation: Take her and go , for I do not suppose I can persuade thee .

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: κτείνειν γ ’ ὃν ἂν χρῇ ; τοῦτο γὰρ τετάγμεθα .
sections.translation: To slay my rightful victim ? Why , that is my appointed task .

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ τοῖς μέλλουσι θάνατον ἐμβαλεῖν .
sections.translation: Nay , but to lay thy deadly hand on those who soon would die .

sections.label: Section 51
sections.original: ἔχω λόγον δὴ καὶ προθυμίαν σέθεν .
sections.translation: I see thy drift , thy eager plea .

sections.label: Section 52
sections.original: ἔστ ’ οὖν ὅπως Ἄλκηστις ἐς γῆρας μόλοι ;
sections.translation: Is it then possible that Alcestis should attain old age ?

sections.label: Section 53
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · τιμαῖς κἀμὲ τέρπεσθαι δόκει .
sections.translation: It is not possible ; I too , methinks , find a pleasure in my rights .

sections.label: Section 54
sections.original: οὔτοι πλέον γ ’ ἂν ἢ μίαν ψυχὴν λάβοις .
sections.translation: Thou canst not anyhow take more than one life .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: νέων φθινόντων μεῖζον ἄρνυμαι γέρας .
sections.translation: When young lives die I reap a higher honour .

sections.label: Section 56
sections.original: κἂν γραῦς ὄληται , πλουσίως ταφήσεται .
sections.translation: Should she die old , a sumptuous funeral will she have .

sections.label: Section 57
sections.original: πρὸς τῶν ἐχόντων , Φοῖβε , τὸν νόμον τίθης .
sections.translation: Phoebus , the law thou layest down is all in favour of the rich .

sections.label: Section 58
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἀλλ ’ ἦ καὶ σοφὸς λέληθας ὤν ;
sections.translation: What mean ’ st thou ? art so wise , and I never knew it ?

sections.label: Section 59
sections.original: ὠνοῖντ ’ ἂν οἷς πάρεστι γηραιοὺς θανεῖν .
sections.translation: Those who have wealth would buy the chance of their dying old .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: οὔκουν δοκεῖ σοι τήνδε μοι δοῦναι χάριν ;
sections.translation: It seems then thou wilt not grant me this favour .

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ · ἐπίστασαι δὲ τοὺς ἐμοὺς τρόπους .
sections.translation: Not I ; my customs well thou knowest .

sections.label: Section 62
sections.original: ἐχθρούς γε θνητοῖς καὶ θεοῖς στυγουμένους .
sections.translation: That I do , customs men detest and gods abhor .

sections.label: Section 63
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δύναιο πάντ ’ ἔχειν ἃ μή σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: Thou canst not realise every lawless wish .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: ἦ μὴν σὺ παύσῃ καίπερ ὠμὸς ὢν ἄγαν ·
sections.translation: Mark me , thou shalt have a check for all thy excessive fierceness ;

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: τοῖος Φέρητος εἶσι πρὸς δόμους ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: such a hero shall there come to Pheres ’ halls , by Eurystheus sent to fetch a team of steeds from the wintry world of Thrace ; he , a guest awhile in these halls of Admetus , will wrest this woman from thee by sheer force .

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: κοὔθ ’ ἡ παρ ’ ἡμῶν σοι γενήσεται χάρις
sections.translation: So wilt thou get no thanks from me but yet wilt do this all the same , and earn my hatred too . [*]

sections.label: Section 72
sections.original: πόλλ ’ ἂν σὺ λέξας οὐδὲν ἂν πλέον λάβοις ·
sections.translation: Thou wilt not gain thy purpose any the more for all thy many words ; that woman shall to Hades ’ halls go down , I tell thee .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἱερὸς γὰρ οὗτος τῶν κατὰ χθονὸς θεῶν
sections.translation: Lo ! I am going for her , that with the sword I may begin my rites , for he whose hair this sword doth hallow is sacred to the gods below .

sections.label: Section 77
sections.original: — τί ποθ ’ ἡσυχία πρόσθεν μελάθρων ;
sections.translation: What [*] means this silence in front of the palace ? why is the house of Admetus stricken dumb ?

sections.label: Section 79
sections.original: — ἀλλ ’ οὐδὲ φίλων πέλας οὐδείς ,
sections.translation: Not one friend near

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ὅστις ἂν εἴποι πότερον φθιμένην
sections.translation: to say if we must mourn our queen as dead , or if she liveth yet and sees the sun , Alcestis , daughter of Pelias , by me and all esteemed the best of wives

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: πόσιν εἰς αὑτῆς γεγενῆσθαι .
sections.translation: to her husband .

sections.label: Section 1.86
sections.original: — κλύει τις ἢ στεναγμὸν ἢ
sections.translation: Doth any of you hear a groan , or sound of hands that smite together , or the voice of lamentation , telling all is over and done ? Yet is there no servant

sections.label: Section 1.90
sections.original: τίζεται ἀμφὶ πύλας . εἰ
sections.translation: stationed about the gate , no , not one . O come , thou saving god , to smooth the swelling waves of woe ! [*]

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: — οὔ τἂν φθιμένης γ ’ ἐσιώπων .
sections.translation: Surely , were she dead , they would not be so still .

sections.label: Section 94
sections.original: — νέκυς ἤδη .
sections.translation: Maybe her corpse is not yet from the house borne forth .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: — πόθεν ; οὐκ αὐχῶ . τί σε θαρσύνει ;
sections.translation: Whence that inference ? I am not so sanguine . What gives thee confidence ?

sections.label: Section 96
sections.original: — πῶς ἂν ἔρημον τάφον Ἄδμητος
sections.translation: How could Admetus let his noble wife go unattended to the grave ?

sections.label: Section 1.98
sections.original: — πυλῶν πάροιθε δ ’ οὐχ ὁρῶ
sections.translation: Before the gates I see no lustral water from the spring , as custom doth ordain should be

sections.label: Section 1.100
sections.original: χέρνιβ ’ ἐπὶ φθιτῶν πύλαις .
sections.translation: at the gates of the dead , no shorn lock lies on the threshold , which , as thou knowest , falls in mourning for the dead , no choir of maidens smites its youthful [*] palms together .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: — καὶ μὴν τόδε κύριον ἦμαρ . . .
sections.translation: And yet this is the appointed day .

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: — τί τόδ ’ αὐδᾷς ;
sections.translation: What meanest thou by this ?

sections.label: Section 107
sections.original: — ᾧ χρή σφε μολεῖν κατὰ γαίας .
sections.translation: The day appointed for the journey to the world below .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: — ἔθιγες ψυχᾶς , ἔθιγες δὲ φρενῶν .
sections.translation: Thou hast touched me to the heart , e ’ en to the soul .

sections.label: Section 109
sections.original: — χρὴ τῶν ἀγαθῶν διακναιομένων
sections.translation: Whoso from his youth up has been accounted virtuous ,

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: πενθεῖν ὅστις
sections.translation: needs must weep to see the good suddenly cut off .

sections.label: Section 2.112
sections.original: — ἀλλ ’ οὐδὲ ναυκληρίαν
sections.translation: ’ Tis done ; no single spot in all the world remains whither one might steer a course , either to Lycia [*]

sections.label: Section 2.115
sections.original: εἴτ ’ ἐπὶ τὰς ἀνύδρους
sections.translation: or to the parched abodes [*] of Ammon to release the hapless lady ’ s soul ; on comes death with step abrupt ,
